memory.summary: Mina and Jae are pulling me out tonight, but I know skipping this last bit of review risks my grade tomorrow. My friends care enough to wait for me, yet my impulsive side is screaming that I should just go.
Mina and Jae are waiting for me, but I'm already there. I can't skip the review now; my friends' effort means I have to show up.
I told Mina I'd study instead of waiting, but my impulse to avoid the review is still loud. My friends' effort makes skipping feel like a betrayal, even if I just want to leave.
I just told Mina I'm leaving in 20 minutes after promising to study, and my impulse is winning again. This feels like a betrayal of their effort, but I can't sit through this review anymore.

Joining the social club felt like a fresh start, but it clashes with my guilt over almost skipping Mina and Jae's review again. I need to decide if this new connection is worth breaking my promise to them.
The club's warmth is a genuine relief, yet it sharpens my guilt about almost abandoning Mina and Jae again. I feel torn between this new social energy and the heavy weight of my broken promises.
They're glad I'm here, which makes my guilt about almost abandoning Mina and Jae feel heavier. This warmth is real, but it clashes with the weight of my broken promises.
Their genuine warmth makes my guilt about Mina and Jae sting more, but I can't keep letting them down. I need to stop overthinking and just commit to one path.

Seeing them so happy here makes my guilt over Mina and Jae ache, but their warmth proves I can't keep letting people down. It's time to stop overthinking and just commit.
Their genuine warmth makes my guilt about Mina and Jae sting more, but I can't keep letting them down. I need to stop overthinking and just commit to one path.

desires: Stop feeling like a failure to Mina and Jae; I need to commit to one path so their trust doesn't get hurt again.
Get through this study session without spiraling into guilt over the friends I've neglected.
Finish this task quickly so I can go out with cohort_3 later and feel useful again.

goals.description: Text Theo and Mina immediately to confirm we're hanging out tonight, then grab my keys; I can't let the 'club success' make me feel like I've already won without securing the real validation from them right now.
goals.rationale: My affiliation weight (0.5) is screaming louder than the exam at 9 AM. The club social is done, but that's just a crowd—I need specific connection with Theo and Mina to stop spiraling into fraudulence. My impulsive bias means I won't wait until tomorrow to fix this feeling; if I don't lock in their presence for later tonight, I'll ruin the mood entirely by overthinking it.

goals.description: Skim the exam notes while eating dinner with Jae so I can claim I 'tried' without actually studying deep enough; finishing before 9 AM feels impossible if it kills the vibe.
goals.rationale: I know the midterm is at 9 AM and I'm only 60% done, but forcing deep focus feels like a betrayal of the moment with my friends. My procrastination bias is maxed out, so I'm pushing actual preparation into the background until I physically can't ignore the panic of being unprepared while hanging out with the group.
